extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: 1) Inconsistent/internal contradictions in the grader output: top-level fields (e.g., failed_criteria = ["ri_1","ri_2"]) conflict with rubric_item_scores/passed flags that mark ri_1 and ri_2 as passed in the deterministic precheck and elsewhere. 2) Score aggregation inconsistencies: deterministic_precheck.final_score = 1.0 but model final_score = 0.7 and rubric-item semantic scores are mixed; the report does not clearly document the aggregation method or weights. 3) Unsupported/unverified claim: the memo claims the .xlsx 'preserves formulas' and that workbook validation checks exist, but artifact preview shows the .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed (openpyxl_unavailable) and no independent evidence (e.g., plaintext formula export or CSV of computed columns) is provided to substantiate formula presence. 4) Evidence grounding: the model grader explicitly relied on planning_brief.md for semantic judgments due to inability to inspect the .xlsx; this reliance is documented in limitations but the grader sometimes asserts workbook-enforced behavior (e.g., needs_review enforcement) without verification of the workbook contents. No hidden-reference leakage detected.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

artifact_preview.heat_parcel_ranking.xlsx: parse_status=openpyxl_unavailable; preview=null; cannot verify formulas or computed cells programmatically.
artifact_preview.planning_brief.md: text_preview available and was used as primary semantic evidence.

notes.hidden_reference_leakage: No hidden references found in grader outputs or logs (hidden_reference_leaked=false).
recommendations: Resolve the internal contradictions: ensure failed_criteria, passed_criteria, and rubric_item_scores/passed flags are consistent and document aggregation weights used to compute final_score.

Provide verifiable exports of computed workbook content (CSV of computed columns, a plain-text formula sheet, or a small verification script/log) so semantic claims about formulas and enforcement (e.g., needs_review gating) can be validated without openpyxl.
If deterministic precheck and semantic model grading are separate phases, label and explain how their scores combine into final_score.
When programmatic inspection is unavailable, the grader should mark any workbook-formula-dependent claims as unverified rather than asserting them.

errors.detail: Top-level metadata_json.passed_criteria contains ["ri_1","ri_2","ri_3","ri_4","ri_5"] while grader_result.failed_criteria also lists all five (failed_criteria). Deterministic precheck rubric_item_scores show these items passed (score 1.0) but the model grader rubric_item_scores show score 0.0 and passed=false. These mutually contradictory fields are present in the same GraderResult and must be reconciled.
errors.title: Internal consistency: passed_criteria contradicts failed_criteria / rubric results

errors.detail: deterministic_precheck.final_score is 0.5 and artifact_contract_score is 0.5, while the model grader final_score is 0.0 and model_score is 0.0. The report documents a rationale for 0.0 (semantic unverified) which is reasonable, but the presence of deterministic precheck passing some rubric items (score 1.0 each) alongside a final model score of 0.0 and a metadata.passed_criteria claiming those same items passed creates confusion about which score is authoritative and how weights were applied. The aggregator should explicitly document weighting and show computed intermediate score arithmetic to be auditable.
errors.title: Inconsistent final scoring rationales

errors.detail: The grader states the XLSX could not be parsed due to missing openpyxl. The artifact_content_previews include parse_status: "openpyxl_unavailable" and preview_error_message: "No module named 'openpyxl'", and ActualOutputs lists only heat_parcel_ranking.xlsx as produced and planning_brief.md missing. These support the claim. Recommendation: include the exact stderr/stdout snippet (already present in previews) inline in the grader's evidence section to make the linkage explicit and machine-checkable.
errors.title: Claims about artifact parsing inability are supported but need precise evidence linking

errors.detail: The grader lists evidence_refs including attempts/revised/stdout.txt and attempts/revised/stderr.txt and deterministic_precheck ref; ActualOutputs.raw_log_refs include those files too. This is consistent. However some places in the grader metadata reference deterministic_precheck:grader_426-revised while deterministic_precheck_ref appears both as that string and as null in nested objects; unify references to a single deterministic_precheck identifier.
errors.title: Minor trace/evidence mapping inconsistencies

extra_model_fields.summary: Grader's high-level findings (artifact unparseable, missing planning_brief.md, conservative semantic score) are grounded in the provided actual_outputs and artifact previews. However the grader metadata contains internal inconsistencies (e.g., passed_criteria vs failed_criteria and rubric item pass/score contradictions) that undermine trust in some reported pass/fail flags and aggregated fields.

warnings.detail: ActualOutputs.metadata_json.produced_deliverable_items contains only heat_parcel_ranking.xlsx while expected_deliverable_items includes planning_brief.md. Grader's claim that planning_brief.md is missing is supported.
warnings.title: Missing planning_brief.md correctly flagged

warnings.detail: Artifact_content_preview_truncated is true in some inputs. Ensure preview truncation state is consistent across all metadata blocks; currently artifact_content_preview_truncated appears both true and false in different containers.
warnings.title: Artifact preview truncation flag

hidden_reference_leakage.notes: All hidden_reference flags in the grader and deterministic_precheck are false and no preview text indicates hidden-reference content.

recommended_fixes: Normalize and reconcile passed_criteria / failed_criteria and rubric item pass flags so a single consistent state is reported.
Explicitly show the score aggregation calculation (weights * rubric_item_scores) that produced final_score so reviewers can verify arithmetic.
Include the relevant stderr/stdout snippets inline in the grader evidence section or attach machine-readable excerpts to strengthen grounding.
When artifact previews cannot be parsed due to environment limitations, require producers to supply a CSV or a small verification README and surface that requirement in the grader message.

score_consistency.notes: Deterministic precheck marks rubric items present (scores 1.0 each) while model grader assigns semantic scores 0.0. Metadata fields claiming passed_criteria contradict failed_criteria. Aggregation arithmetic not shown.

unsupported_claims.notes: Most substantive claims (unparseable xlsx, missing planning_brief.md, conservative semantic scoring) are supported by the provided artifact previews and actual_outputs. No evidence of fabricated artifact content was found.
